mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the cross-sectional Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, persistent inflation readings force the market to price in prolonged restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the rate threshold breach, or forward return spread is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in real personal savings reflects rising household balance sheet strength, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where RSAFS rises consecutively for two months.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment growth accelerates while industrial production stagnates indicates a structural shift toward services, which historically compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the cross-sectional Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 100, it signals a severe supply-side contraction that typically precedes earnings downgrades, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the industrial production threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where housing starts exceed 1400 thousand units, rising construction activity amplifies cyclical earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The housing-driven signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances during late-cycle phases, or the forward return spread between high and low housing periods is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
